user_input: How can I apply for student accommodation at Sheffield Hallam University?
retrieved_contexts:
["How to apply for undergraduate courses at Sheffield Hallam University:

Application route:
- Apply through UCAS (Universities and Colleges Admissions Service) at ucas.com
- SHU UCAS institution code: S21
- Most undergraduate courses: apply by January 29 each year (Equal Consideration Deadline)
- Art, design and performance courses: check individual course deadlines
- Late applications: accepted after January deadline if places remain

UCAS application steps:
1. Register at ucas.com and create your account
2. Search for your course using SHU's UCAS code S21
3. Write your personal statement (4,000 characters, explains why you want to study the subject)
4. Get a reference from a teacher or advisor
5. Pay UCAS application fee (£28.50 for multiple choices, £24 for single choice)
6. Submit your application

After applying:
- SHU will send a decision: unconditional offer, conditional offer, or rejection
- Conditional offers: typically require specific A-level grades or equivalent
- Accept your firm choice by the UCAS reply deadline (usually May)
- Contextual offers: SHU may make lower offers to students from disadvantaged backgrounds via Hallam Progress

Clearing:
- Priority Clearing opens July 2026
- Available if you don't hold an offer, don't meet conditions, or are applying after deadline
- Call SHU's Clearing hotline: 0114 225 5550 (from July)
- Visit shu.ac.uk/clearing for current vacancies

Entry requirements:
- Vary by course — check individual course pages
- Typical UG range: 96–144 UCAS points (roughly BBC to AAA at A-level)
- Equivalent qualifications accepted: BTEC, T-Levels, Access to HE, Scottish Highers, Irish Leaving Certificate, IB
- English language: GCSE Grade 4 (C) or equivalent required for home students

Transfer students:
- Can apply to transfer from another university — email enquiries@shu.ac.uk
- Credit transfer from prior learning may be considered", 'Sheffield Hallam University halls of residence (2025/26):

GUARANTEE:
All full-time undergraduate students who firm SHU as their first choice are GUARANTEED an offer of accommodation in halls for their first year.

OVERVIEW:
- 11 halls of residence properties
- All within walking distance of both campuses
- All bills included (gas, electricity, water, Wi-Fi, contents insurance)
- No hidden costs
- Weekly prices: £81 – £185 per week (2025/26)
- All registered with the Snug housing scheme (quality standard)
- 24-hour security and residential support team
- Applications open for September 2026 intake

STANDARD ROOM FEATURES:
- Single bed with mattress
- Wardrobe and drawers
- Desk and chair
- En-suite or shared bathroom (varies by property)
- Communal kitchen and lounge areas
- Laundry facilities on site
- Secure entry systems

APPLYING:
- Apply online at: accom-online.shu.ac.uk
- Apply as soon as you receive an offer from SHU
- You do not need to sign contracts or pay deposit until results confirmed

user_input: How do I contact the admissions team at Sheffield Hallam University?
retrieved_contexts:
["Sheffield Hallam University key contacts (March 2026):

GENERAL ENQUIRIES:
- Main switchboard: +44 (0)114 225 5555
- General email: enquiries@shu.ac.uk
- Address: City Campus, Howard Street, Sheffield, S1 1WB

STUDENT SERVICES (Hallam Help):
- Phone: 0114 225 4321 (Mon–Fri 9am–5pm)
- Email: hallamhelp@shu.ac.uk
- Location: Owen Building, City Campus (ground floor)
- Online: hallamhelp.shu.ac.uk (MyHallam portal)

ADMISSIONS:
- Undergraduate: 0114 225 5555
- Postgraduate: pg.admissions@shu.ac.uk
- International admissions: international.admissions@shu.ac.uk

INTERNATIONAL STUDENTS:
- International Experience Team: international.experience@shu.ac.uk
- Visa / immigration advice: immigration.adviser@shu.ac.uk

ACCOMMODATION:
- Accommodation office: accommodation@shu.ac.uk
- Online applications: accom-online.shu.ac.uk

FINANCE:
- Student Finance Team: studentfinance@shu.ac.uk
- Scholarship enquiries: funding@shu.ac.uk

WELLBEING & SUPPORT:
- Wellbeing Services: wellbeing@shu.ac.uk | 0114 225 3333
- Disability Support: disabledstudenthallam@shu.ac.uk | 0114 225 2222
- Careers: careers@shu.ac.uk | 0114 225 3900

SECURITY (24 hours):
- Emergency (on-campus): 0114 225 4444
- Non-emergency security: 0114 225 2012

STUDENTS' UNION:
- Website: hallamstudentsunion.com
- Student advice: student.advice@hallamstudentsunion.com

EMERGENCY SERVICES (Sheffield):
- Emergency: 999
- NHS urgent care: 111
- Royal Hallamshire Hospital A&E: 0114 271 1900
- Northern General Hospital A&E: 0114 243 4343
- Police non-emergency: 101

SOCIAL MEDIA:
- Instagram: @sheffhallamuni
- Facebook: /sheffieldhallamuniversity
- YouTube: /sheffieldhallamuni", "How to apply for undergraduate courses at Sheffield Hallam University:

Application route:
- Apply through UCAS (Universities and Colleges Admissions Service) at ucas.com
- SHU UCAS institution code: S21
- Most undergraduate courses: apply by January 29 each year (Equal Consideration Deadline)
- Art, design and performance courses: check individual course deadlines
- Late applications: accepted after January deadline if places remain

UCAS application steps:
1. Register at ucas.com and create your account
2. Search for your course using SHU's UCAS code S21
3. Write your personal statement (4,000 characters, explains why you want to study the subject)
4. Get a reference from a teacher or advisor
5. Pay UCAS application fee (£28.50 for multiple choices, £24 for single choice)
6. Submit your application

After applying:
- SHU will send a decision: unconditional offer, conditional offer, or rejection
- Conditional offers: typically require specific A-level grades or equivalent
- Accept your firm choice by the UCAS reply deadline (usually May)
- Contextual offers: SHU may make lower offers to students from disadvantaged backgrounds via Hallam Progress

Clearing:
- Priority Clearing opens July 2026
- Available if you don't hold an offer, don't meet conditions, or are applying after deadline
- Call SHU's Clearing hotline: 0114 225 5550 (from July)
- Visit shu.ac.uk/clearing for current vacancies

Entry requirements:
- Vary by course — check individual course pages
- Typical UG range: 96–144 UCAS points (roughly BBC to AAA at A-level)
- Equivalent qualifications accepted: BTEC, T-Levels, Access to HE, Scottish Highers, Irish Leaving Certificate, IB
- English language: GCSE Grade 4 (C) or equivalent required for home students

Transfer students:
- Can apply to transfer from another university — email enquiries@shu.ac.uk
- Credit transfer from prior learning may be considered", "City Campus is the main Sheffield Hallam campus, located in the heart of Sheffield city centre.

Address: Howard Street, Sheffield, S1 1WB

Key buildings:
- Owen Building — main teaching building, lecture theatres
- Adsetts Centre — main library, open 24 hours during term time, 1,200+ study spaces
- Eric Mensforth Building — engineering labs, workshops
- Heart of the Campus (Heart) — student hub, café, social spaces
- Hallam Square — outdoor social space, events
- Cantor Building — art, design, creative facilities
- Robert Winston Building — science labs
- Pennine Lecture Theatre — large capacity events

Facilities:
- Multiple cafés and restaurants including Hallam Café
- Hallam Help desk (student services, enrolment, ID cards)
- Students' Union (new HQ opening 2025/26)
- Sport Hallam gym
- On-site pharmacy and medical centre nearby
- 24-hour security

Transport:
- Sheffield Supertram: City Hall stop (2 min walk)
- Sheffield train station: 10 min walk
- Bus: multiple routes serve Howard Street
- Parking: limited on-site, NCP car parks nearby"]
response:
You can contact the admissions team at Sheffield Hallam University as follows:

- Undergraduate admissions: 0114 225 5555
- Postgraduate admissions: pg.admissions@shu.ac.uk
- International admissions: international.admissions@shu.ac.uk

Source: https://www.shu.ac.uk/contact-us
reference: To contact Sheffield Hallam University: General enquiries — main switchboard: +44 (0)114 225 5555, email: enquiries@shu.ac.uk, address: City Campus, Howard Street, Sheffield, S1 1WB. Student Services (Hallam Help) — phone: 0114 225 4321 (Mon–Fri 9am–5pm), email: hallamhelp@shu.ac.uk, online: hallamhelp.shu.ac.uk. Undergraduate admissions: 0114 225 5555. Postgraduate admissions: pg.admissions@shu.ac.uk. International admissions: international.admissions@shu.ac.uk.

user_input: Tell me about Sheffield Hallam University.
retrieved_contexts:
['Sheffield Hallam University (SHU) is one of the UK's largest universities, located in Sheffield, South Yorkshire.

Key facts (verified March 2026):
- Founded: 1843 (as Sheffield School of Design), gained university status 1992
- Students: approx. 34,000 students including 5,000+ international students
- Staff: approx. 4,000
- TEF Rating: Gold (2023 Teaching Excellence Framework)
- QS Stars: 5-Star Excellence Rating
- Ranking: Top 10 UK student city (Uni Compare 2025)
- Graduate outcomes: 95% of graduates in work or further study (Graduate Outcomes Survey 2022/23)
- UCAS institution code: S21

Campuses:
1. City Campus — Howard Street, Sheffield, S1 1WB (main campus, city centre)
2. Collegiate Campus — Psalter Lane, Sheffield, S11 8UZ (south of city)
3. Sheffield Hallam in London — Brent Cross Town (new campus opening)

Contact:
- General enquiries: +44 (0)114 225 5555
- Email: enquiries@shu.ac.uk
- Website: www.shu.ac.uk
- MyHallam portal: myhallam.shu.ac.uk

Mission: "Learn and Serve" — committed to transforming lives through education and research.', "City Campus is the main Sheffield Hallam campus, located in the heart of Sheffield city centre.

Address: Howard Street, Sheffield, S1 1WB

Key buildings:
- Owen Building — main teaching building, lecture theatres
- Adsetts Centre — main library, open 24 hours during term time, 1,200+ study spaces
- Eric Mensforth Building — engineering labs, workshops
- Heart of the Campus (Heart) — student hub, café, social spaces
- Hallam Square — outdoor social space, events
- Cantor Building — art, design, creative facilities
- Robert Winston Building — science labs
- Pennine Lecture Theatre — large capacity events

Facilities:
- Multiple cafés and restaurants including Hallam Café

- Sport Hallam gym
- On-site pharmacy and medical centre nearby
- 24-hour security

Transport:
- Sheffield Supertram: City Hall stop (2 min walk)
- Sheffield train station: 10 min walk
- Bus: multiple routes serve Howard Street
- Parking: limited on-site, NCP car parks nearby", 'Sheffield Hallam University awards and rankings (2025/26):

Teaching:
- TEF Gold rating (2023) — highest possible rating for teaching quality and student outcomes
- 91% postgraduate computing students satisfied with teaching (PG Taught Satisfaction Survey 2025)

Student experience:
- Top 10 UK student city — Uni Compare 2025
- 5-Star QS Excellence Rating
- Sheffield ranked one of the most affordable student cities in the UK (NatWest Student Living Index 2023)

Graduate outcomes:
- 95% graduates in work or further study within 15 months of graduating
- 94% UK graduates employed or in further study (2022/23 Graduate Outcomes Survey)

Research:
- Multiple REF-rated research centres
- Award for Outstanding Research Environment (AWRC) in sport and physical activity
- Helena Kennedy Centre for International Justice (nationally recognised in law and criminology)

response:
Sheffield Hallam University (SHU) is one of the UK's largest universities, located in Sheffield, South Yorkshire. Founded in 1843 as the Sheffield School of Design, it gained university status in 1992. SHU has approximately 34,000 students, including over 5,000 international students, and around 4,000 staff members. It has received a Gold rating in the 2023 Teaching Excellence Framework (TEF) and a 5-Star Excellence Rating from QS Stars. The university is ranked among the top 10 UK student cities by Uni Compare 2025, and 95% of its graduates are in work or further study within 15 months of graduating.

SHU has three campuses: the main City Campus in the heart of Sheffield, the Collegiate Campus in the south of the city, and a new campus opening in London at Brent Cross Town.

Source: https://www.shu.ac.uk/about-us/who-we-are
reference: Sheffield Hallam University (SHU) is one of the UK's largest universities, located in Sheffield, South Yorkshire. Key facts: Founded in 1843 as the Sheffield School of Design, gained university status in 1992. Approximately 34,000 students including 5,000+ international students, and around 4,000 staff. TEF Rating: Gold (2023 Teaching Excellence Framework). QS Stars: 5-Star Excellence Rating. Ranked in the Top 10 UK student cities (Uni Compare 2025). 95% of graduates are in work or further study within 15 months (Graduate Outcomes Survey 2022/23). UCAS institution code: S21. Two main campuses: City Campus at Howard Street, Sheffield S1 1WB, and Collegiate Campus at Psalter Lane, Sheffield S11 8UZ. General enquiries: +44 (0)114 225 5555 or enquiries@shu.ac.uk.
